reviews.review_description: I'm a pilot with 50 years of experience, and I'll say: this simulator rocks. I've worked with many simulators on many aircraft types, but only here, when I come back from a mission, I catch myself wanting to rest my hands on the cockpit sides. I don't mind at all that it practically has no minimum speed – I take care of the speed myself. I don't know how much I'd give for separate rudder pedals – operated by feet, of course. I must add: I'm a noob, but the app handles, for example, the Cessna Rudder Pro perfectly. Thank you, oh developer, for giving me this wonderful, atmospheric flight simulator, in these beautiful old aircraft, which, on top of that, respond beautifully to the rudder.

Me personally, I find the flight mechanics in this game amazing, the ai isn’t too difficult, nor too easy. The campaign is amazing, and quick play is nice too, there’s just one thing.

No one plays the online modes, I can never find an open session.

It’s an amazing game yes, but it gets pretty lonely.

In this game, I have a lot of fun but after like an hour of playing it just got kind of easy so I give it four stars cause it’s a little bit too easy. You should make it a little bit harder, but another reason is like a plane just out turns me like I can’t turn how it turned and that’s my review but it is pretty fun. ￼

reviews.review_title: Brings Back the days of Red Baron
reviews.review_description:
This flight Simulator I imaged I was playing in 1994.  I would like to see more mission variants added such as a career mode with contemporary planes based on timeline and entry point in the war...with promotion systems etc in a career mode like the game of old.   Also given it had you posted in certsin locations and was an opportunity to learn about history one mission variant should return being the incident where you habe to fight off enemy fighters from your base in a ground up start vs the opposite you hitting them at their aerodrome.  I'd pay for the expansion if need be.....this is my favorite VR game so far. 
As for how to succeed attack from elevation, use the energy for speed and straff planes on your way down then pull and Immellmann and do it all again.
I have the World War II, World War I games and they’re great. I love them, but where is Vietnam and Korea?
people don't play a multiplayer even though multiplayer so good

developer_response.body:
Our community comes from different time zones, so it may also be a good idea to check the Private Lobby, thanks to which you can start a multiplayer game with your friends at any time. It is also worth considering joining our discord:
https://discord.gg/ntPmutN

Where there is always another pilot you can fly with. It is also worth mentioning that multiplayer is cross platform, so you won't be limited just to one platform.

There are also FB groups for VR fans of the Warplanes trilogy, where they regularly meet in closed games, but are also eager to welcome new members interested in playing together.

It's you vs 6/7/8/9/10 + enemies. You get f all money for completing missions. In one mission you immediately get blasted by 3 ships leaving you with little health while you have to fight endless enemies. 

It's just a bad game. not enjoyable.

developer_response.body:
Generally, in the case of Warplanes: WW1 Fighters, you "learn as you go" and the game itself guides you via relevant messages. "Game Tips" (available while in base, by clicking on the '?' on the board) are also worth checking.

There are, however, a few guides on our webpage that you can check in addition to this. Please check this link: 
https://www.homenetgames.com/warplanes-ww1-fighters/
At the bottom, you will find the "Guides and features" section. Same with Warplanes: Battles over Pacific on its dedicated webpage and Warplanes: Air Corp.

Also, please make sure that you are making proper use of your Squadron. We have prepared a guide that might be helpful:
https://www.homenetgames.com/warplanes-battles-over-pacific/article/bop-squadron-guide/ 
UI for Warplanes: WW1 Fighters is similar.

What could be helpful is playing in "Arcade" mode, where you can always use auto-aim by holding the left analog stick before firing. You can always change the currently selected game mode on the middle board while at base, checking the mission description, using the arrows right below the START button.

If you need money but you can't finish any current campaign missions, then you can try to play multiplayer to earn money for new planes.
Also, when you are playing the campaign, remember that you can switch between your planes and use them better than AI pilots.
It is also worth noting that you can transfer funds between campaigns via the gifting option.

Lastly, our support would be happy to answer any of your precise questions via https://www.homenetgames.com/support/

https://www.homenetgames.com/support/

I figured out how to play co-op on a campaign. This was difficult to figure out, but I kept playing around and got it. The developer's response is incorrect. My instructions are included below.

INSTRUCTIONS

1. Go to campaign! (Not multiplayer)

2. Play the campaign until you have at least a plane with an extra seat or a second plane unlocked. (The latter is recommended)

3. When in campaign mode, select (at the top right) Legacy Multiplayer!!! You can now create a private session using only the planes you have. As you complete missions you can use those funds to buy new planes. (You do have to leave the session to buy the new planes, but then when you put up your new session the new planes are there)

4. The nice thing about legacy multiplayer is that you can decide on the number of respawns making it more challenging to complete a full set of however many missions you pick. Whereas in the regular multiplayer you can spawn as much as you'd like making it just a charge, die, and compete for kills competition.

developer_response.body:
Step-by-step guide to Co-Op play:

Host steps
1. Start the game, select "Multiplayer".
2. Create a private lobby, set its name, and give it to your partner.
3. Select "Create Session" and "Co-Op", choose single mission (or "Raid" mode if you'd like to play multiple in a row), and confirm with "Create".
4. Choose a plane (mark it as a favorite) that you want to start with, from the right board. If you are interested in two-seaters, there are multiple possibilities like: Entente's DH.4 & DH.10 and Central Powers Albatros C.III & AGO C.I.
5. Wait until your partner joins the lobby.

Player steps
1. Select "Multiplayer" from the left board.
2. Select the private lobby, enter the name the host gave you, and select "Join Lobby".
3. You will see the plane selected by the Host in the aircraft list, select the gunner icon to join the aircraft crew.

Really fun game but can you tell me how to do takeoffs and landings in war mode? I don't see anything in the settings that let's me do this

developer_response.body:
After launching the game and starting your chosen campaign in 'Real War' mode, you'll be greeted with a prologue, after which you'll be able to land. Each subsequent mission you undertake in this mode will begin with a takeoff at the airport and a landing after completing the mission. 

Additionally, after completing the prologue, while at base, just before starting a new mission and clicking "START!" on the middle board, you can always use the < > arrows to change game modes. By selecting Custom Mode and using the gear between the arrows, you'll be able to customize the parameters further, including takeoffs and landings.

reviews.review_title: How is this not a store title?
reviews.review_description: Fantastic game , don't be put off by the possible motion sickness at the beginning. reviews.review_description:
I love playing this. Yes, the missions are totally repetitive but, be honest, there probably wasn't a lot of variety in ww1  reality. Attack a convoy, intercept the enemy, defend the base etc. What's important is that they're fun. 
I also had some motion sickness when I started playing it but I got over it. 1st time in ages I've suffered any and I'm glad it didn't last long.
I find I rarely use most of the plants having found a great all-rounder on each side. There's rarely any point to having the others as anything other than wingmen. It's useful to have difficulty levels but I find full simulation mode almost unplayable as the controls are far too sensitive. I go from being a great pilot to an awful one. Having no force feedback, or any feedback, makes it wildly over responsive and hard tob gauge.
My most played game of the moment.

For someone who always wanted to learn to fly, who is fascinated by WW I air combat and has dreamt of putting those together and getting into dogfights, bombing runs, and taking down dirigibles…you can’t ask for more, this game hits on all cylinders.
This game is good that’s why the title of my review is :D
I like the game 🎮
It would be better if you added explosions when the zeppelins go down. And to have more maps to select from.

A 1v15 with 4 of them being bombers? the only way to get money is making it atleast simulation difficulty but good luck getting railed by 8 fighters at once. No amount of turning,flips or swirls is stopping the auto aim from the bots.

Why are the enemies so much faster than me?!?! Why do I burnout after only 8 seconds of acceleration?so frustrating to watch them fly by in real planes while I sit in a giant block in the sky. By the time I turned towards them, they’re flying past me. ￼Stupid stupid stupid. I was already having a horrible day and this makes WW3 look appealing. Garbage game and garbage studio. I must admit, I wasn’t expecting much from this game given the low price, but I was instantly surprised at the level of realism and the breadth of content. The controls are intuitive and simple, and the multiplayer is superb. Lots of choice of planes makes it great. Being able to fine-tune the sensitivity of the steering would be ideal, but it’s fine. If you’re looking for a good fighter plane game, this is for you!

Ignore all the people complaining that they get sick from playing, they are too ignorant to turn on the settings that may fix it.

Everyone seems to have only played this for 5 minutes. The campaigns are extremely boring and the AI is garbage. Planes just do endless circles and bombers have extreme accuracy making missions against them impossible. You fly up against 20+ planes with a wingman whose a moron. It would be cool and better if you had more than one to 3 other planes with you. Also I’m currently soft-locked as I don’t have enough money to buy a new plane and my current plane cannot complete the only mission available to me, a base defense mission where I’m the only plane in the air against 20+ airplanes. This game is infuriating and inaccurate as hell. All cockpits are the same. The engine mechanic of it overheating and cooling that quickly is boring and unrealistic. There’s constant clipping with the plane around you. It’s not fun. After you get used to the flying feel it’s not fun. Missions are repetitive and boring.

Yea a squadron of 4 aircraft against an infinite number of fighters is still stupid and way less fun. You still didn’t address the bad AI that can do inhuman loops. Multiple times I saw Ai fighters doing inverted loops which looked like -4 g pulls. The Ai bomber gunners are basically matrix agents with their accuracy which means if you’re in their sights for 1 seconds you’re dead or half your health is gone.

developer_response.body:
If you need money but you can't finish any current campaign missions then you can try to play multiplayer to earn money for new planes.
Also when you are playing the campaign, remember that you can switch between your planes and use them better than AI pilots. 
It is also worth noting that you can transfer funds between campaigns via the gifting option.

You can have more than 3 planes in your squadron, you just have to check your medal distribution and make sure that your "max squadron size" has been upgraded.

reviews.review_title: Great Game but tainted by stupidity
reviews.review_description: Love this game but the VR developers need to grow up. VR is tainted by short, undeveloped overpriced experiences. Sometimes you get a charm of a game. Just like this one.  Then the developer asks you to choose which platform (Oculus or Steam). This has angered me. Why would any small developer want to limit in such a way? No doubt you've thought about a WWII version of this game. Trust me, I will be thinking twice about that due to such a stupid decision. I love VR but it these sort of practices that cause it such harm.

developer_response.body: Would you be so kind as to expand on this thought? What exactly is the problem, because if you mean crossplay between the two indicated platforms, then I hasten to inform you that the game supports it.

reviews.review_description: This game sucks its lame dumb and it's a waste of money .

I will warn you, you will get motion sick. I have found however that the plane your flying will effect how quickly the motion sickness sets in. But regardless of the motion sickness, this game is an absolute BLAST to play, using the plane controls, shooting flares at zeppelin's, dropping bombs on convoys, and boats, this game is so much fun, even more so with friends, I would GLADLY recommend this game!😁👍

I've been putting off this review for quite some time now because I know my own words wont do this game justice. But I'm gonna try anyways.

I've been fascinated by aviation since I was old enough to talk. WW1 aviation specifically holds a strong place in my heart, the thought that we made it into the sky one hundred years ago using wood and canvas amazes me. (So keep in mind I may have a bias opinion)

This game is by far the best purchase I've made on the oculus store so far, and frankly one of the only reasons I put on my headset. The flight physics, while simplistic, allow you to flow smoother than any other dog fighting game I've played.

The graphics are nothing to go crazy about, but when you're 1000-2000 feet off the ground trying to not get shot at the graphics don't look half bad. The moment you get to rest and fly with no planes coming after you, you will appreciate the smaller details they've added.

While the plane list isn't the largest, they give you more than enough to work with.

Lastly, the community. If you can find the regulars, you'll be welcomed with open arms, they love to take noobies under their wing and teaching them every trick they know to become a better dog fighter. While you wont always be thrown in a server full of nice people, and things can get heated in the moment. This game has given me a family so to speak.

If you're looking for a fun/vomit inducing/decent looking/repetitive PVP, or just have a love for aviation or passion for historical aircraft, this is definitely the game for you.

ThePorcoRosso

reviews.review_title: If that is what it was like
reviews.review_description: As an avid reader of WW1 and WW2 history I was thrilled to get an oculus for Xmas. First game had to be this one which immediately made me feel sick such was the movement and height perception it creates. After a few days I didn't need an in flight sick bag and I'm getting more used to how this system works. The visual effects are very good, the reality appears pretty accurate and this is where my heading comes from. If the simulations are accurate, then those pilots were made of pretty bold stuff. Under powered, exposed to every threat possible and vulnerable,  they deserve every accolade possible. This game does not appear to have a practice mode where it makes you use and understand the controls before you "take off" however with perseverance it starts to make sense.  The scenarios are a bit similar but all in all I'm enjoying it and can't wait for WW2 to be brought out. Chocks away.
